selected_goal: Prevent legacy mark-scheme crops from including the next question when the next anchor is near the top of the following page.
why_this_now: S20 P51 question 6 still showed question 7 after preliminary notes were ignored. The remaining issue was a next-anchor page being included even though it contained no continuation of question 6 before the question 7 anchor.
repo_evidence: _blocks_for_legacy_anchor_bounds includes following-page content for legacy answer spans.
_mark_scheme_regions_for_start pads rendered regions, which can reintroduce below-boundary content if the bottom is not clamped.
S20 P51 question 6 is a real 2020 historical paper matching the user's highlighted date range.
acceptance_criteria: A page containing only the next legacy question before any active-question continuation is skipped for the active question.
Rendered mark-scheme crop bottoms are clamped to the computed answer boundary after padding.
S20 P51 question 6 mark-scheme image excludes question 7.
test_expectations: Add a focused unit test where the next anchor is near the top of the following page.
Run the focused test and full question-detection suite.
Run a targeted real S20 P51 processing probe and inspect the q6 mark-scheme image dimensions/content.
implementation_boundaries: Do not remove valid continuation pages where active-question content appears before the next anchor.
Do not reduce all crop padding globally.
Keep this behavior in legacy mark-scheme crop/block logic.
repo_hygiene_risk: Medium: following-page skipping can drop legitimate continuations if the near-top boundary test is too aggressive.
rollback_plan: Remove _next_anchor_page_has_no_legacy_continuation, the bottom clamp adjustment, and the next-anchor-page regression test.
done_when: The combined image-rendering and question-detection tests pass.
The targeted S20 P51 q6 probe shows no q7 contamination.
